differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: No hydration, execution resumes on interaction. Faster initial load.
differences.rightDesc: Renders components efficiently with minimal DOM updates via fine-grained reactivity.

differences.title: 2. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Uses context for state sharing across components, similar to React's approach.
differences.rightDesc: Core state primitives. Updates are tracked and applied granularly.

differences.title: 3. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Little to no JavaScript shipped initially. Executes on demand.
differences.rightDesc: Generates highly optimized, smaller JavaScript bundles for faster parsing.

differences.title: 4. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Achieves near-instant interactivity by avoiding upfront JavaScript execution.
differences.rightDesc: Exceptional performance with efficient rendering and minimal re-renders.

differences.title: 5. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Avoids re-rendering entire components; execution resumes where needed.
differences.rightDesc: Reactivity is at the core, updating only affected parts of the DOM.

differences.title: 6. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Feels familiar to React developers due to JSX and component model.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding signals and reactive primitives for full benefit.

differences.title: 7. ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: Focuses on making components interactive as independent 'islands'.
differences.rightDesc: Compiler optimizes reactivity and DOM updates for peak performance.
